# Code of Conduct

This project has adopted the Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, as its
code of conduct. The full text is maintained by the Contributor Covenant
project and is available at:

- https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/
- Plain-text copy: https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/code_of_conduct.md

By participating in this community (filing issues, submitting pull requests,
commenting, reviewing), you agree to uphold the Contributor Covenant.

## Reporting

To report conduct issues, use one of these channels (in order of preference):

1. **GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting** for issues that also involve a
   security concern — see [`SECURITY.md`](../SECURITY.md).
2. **Direct message to a repository owner** listed in
   [`CODEOWNERS`](../CODEOWNERS) — use their GitHub profile contact or email.
3. **Open a private issue** by contacting a maintainer first; do not post
   conduct reports publicly.

Reports will be handled as outlined in the Contributor Covenant's enforcement
section (linked above). Confidentiality of the reporter is maintained to the
extent possible while addressing the report.

## Scope

This code of conduct applies within all project spaces — issues, pull
requests, review comments, discussions, commit messages, documentation — and
also when an individual is representing the project in public spaces. It does
not govern off-project personal conduct.
